text:
Once upon a time near the edge of a verdant forest, there lived a rabbit named Felix who had a peculiar talent. Felix wasn't just ordinary—a whisper of wind could make him leap higher or run faster—or maybe it was just his cheerful disposition. Every morning, he'd hop to the forest's edge, gaze into the unknown woods, and imagine all the adventures that awaited.

One day, while nibbling on some fresh carrots he'd found near a little brook, Felix met a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver didn't just know everything about the forest. He knew about dreams, the significance of dreams... Oliver was quite philosophical.

"You always hop so enthusiastically," Oliver quivered. "Are dreams exciting? I've seen them come true."

Felix tilted his head. "Really? That sounds incredible!"

"Yes," Oliver chuckled softly. "In the night, under the stars. Sometimes I see a world where the rivers flow upward or forests grow quieter tones. Do those visions ever
